channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Could attack on Turkey, a Nato member, prove costly for Iran?
Oxford Museum is returning a 500-year-old statue to India. Here's why
Fact-check: Is the US using Indian naval bases to attack Iran?
Cooking oil, medicines to become expensive… How the Iran war will impact your wallet
Blinding the US? How Iran destroyed the $1.1-billion American missile radar in Qatar
Has Israel denied Indians access to shelters amid the Iran war?
West Asian crisis: Energy markets face a Hormuz test
item.description: What began as geopolitical tension in the Gulf has quickly become a physical shock to energy markets

item.title: US Sutra | The Iran paradox: The strange case of Left-liberal mourning for Tehran
item.description: To eulogise and lament the demise of a barbarous despot and a brutal regime, which trampled on its own citizens and destroyed its own country economically, socially and politically, is sheer folly

item.title: The sad tale of Pakistan: In trying to be too clever by half, hoisted by its own petard as Iran war escalates
item.description: Pakistan’s strategic overreach, driven largely by its desire to outmanoeuvre India, has instead resulted in a strategic squeeze

item.title: Strategic suicide, not strategic depth: Why Pakistan’s ‘jihadi chickens’ in Afghanistan have come home to roost
item.description: As clashes flare along the Durand Line, Islamabad’s long-touted ‘strategic depth’ doctrine collapses under the weight of its own contradictions

item.title: Iran and the Indian art of strategic silence
item.description: India sees that the Iranian regime is unpopular, and murderous, at home, and its strategic offerings are limited. In a difficult world, it acts accordingly

item.title: Bangladesh’s International Crimes Tribunal: A syndicate in the guise of justice
item.description: Bribery, manufactured immunity, and selective amnesia have transformed Bangladesh's ICT into a protection racket, laundering the guilt of street-level killers to fuel a partisan purge against the Awami League
